codeView.restoreConfirm: Discard edits and restore the original decompiled result?

analysis.noFile: Select a file to view analysis
analysis.noDataHint: Requires a WASM module with decompileRich export
analysis.sourceFile: This file is a source file, no decompilation needed

cfgViewer.focusHint: Focused on {block} ({hops}-hop neighborhood). Double-click another block to re-focus.

tips.dialect: Target Lua dialect, affects parser and generator behavior. CLI: --dialect / -D
tips.generateMode: Error handling strategy. strict: stop on error; best-effort: try alternative syntax; permissive: output as much as possible. CLI: --generate-mode / -g
tips.parseMode: Parser strictness. strict: strict validation; permissive: tolerate non-standard bytecode. CLI: --parse-mode / -p
tips.stringEncoding: Encoding for string constant decoding. CLI: --encoding / -e
tips.stringDecodeMode: Behavior on string decode failure. strict: error; lossy: replace with placeholders. CLI: --decode-mode / -m
tips.returnInlineMaxComplexity: Max inline expression complexity in return statements. CLI: --return-inline-max-complexity
tips.indexInlineMaxComplexity: Max inline expression complexity in table index expressions. CLI: --index-inline-max-complexity
tips.argsInlineMaxComplexity: Max inline expression complexity in call arguments. CLI: --args-inline-max-complexity
tips.accessBaseInlineMaxComplexity: Max inline expression complexity in table access bases. CLI: --access-base-inline-max-complexity
tips.namingMode: Variable naming strategy. debug-like: use debug info; simple: sequential numbers; heuristic: inferred names. CLI: --naming-mode / -n
tips.debugLikeIncludeFunction: In debug-like naming mode, whether to include function-shaped names. CLI: --debug-like-include-function
tips.maxLineLength: Preferred maximum line length for generated code. CLI: --max-line-length
tips.quoteStyle: String quote style. prefer-double: prefer "; prefer-single: prefer '; min-escape: minimize escapes. CLI: --quote-style
tips.tableStyle: Table constructor layout. compact: single line; balanced: auto; expanded: one entry per line. CLI: --table-style
tips.conservativeOutput: Enable conservative output mode for safer but potentially more verbose code. CLI: --conservative-output
tips.comment: Whether to emit comments and metadata in generated code. CLI: --comment

about.title: About
about.summary: Analyze and decompile Lua bytecode directly in your browser
about.description: unluac-web is built on top of unluac-rs and WebAssembly for quickly inspecting decompiled output, comparing results, and drilling into Proto / CFG views when you need more structure.

multiDialect.title: Multi-version, multi-dialect support
multiDialect.description: Covers Lua 5.1–5.5, LuaJIT, and Luau, with parser and generator behavior configurable per target dialect.

localFirst.title: Runs fully on-device
localFirst.description: Decompilation and analysis happen inside the current browser via WebAssembly, without uploading bytecode to a server.

tooling.title: More than plain source output
tooling.description: Alongside source code, you can batch process files, compare outputs, and inspect structured Proto / CFG analysis.

diff.hint: Select two decompiled files to compare
